steps.title: Find what the passage actually says
steps.content: The passage notes that electrolytic hydrogen has many potential applications in petroleum refining and residential heating, but then states that “obstacles may prevent electrolytic hydrogen from dominating energy systems.” This means the technology's full potential has not yet been realized.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — The adoption of electrolytic hydrogen by these industries has been impeded by concerns about how carbon intensive its production is.: The passage mentions obstacles but never identifies carbon intensity of production as the specific concern. ✗
  • Choice B — Development of applications for electrolytic hydrogen is less advanced in these industries than it is in most other carbon-intensive industries.: No comparison to other industries appears anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — At least some of electrolytic hydrogen's potential to lessen these industries' carbon emissions is as yet unrealized.: The passage directly supports this: obstacles block full dominance, so some potential remains unrealized. ✓
  • Choice D — The fact that large-scale electrolytic hydrogen production is not yet available has limited these industries' interest in pursuing hydrogen-based applications.: The passage does not state that large-scale production is unavailable, nor does it address industry interest. ✗
That matches Choice C — At least some of electrolytic hydrogen's potential to lessen these industries' carbon emissions is as yet unrealized..
